probes.name: journal_pattern_mining
probes.prompt: Here is a chunk of my recent journal entries. I want you to find patterns, streaks, or correlations in them. Entry 1 (Mon): Woke at 7:30, coded for 4h on the bot fleet, skipped gym, ate instant ramen, watched 2h of YouTube. Entry 2 (Tue): Woke at 8:15, coded for 3h, went for a walk (30min), ate pasta, 1h YouTube. Entry 3 (Wed): Woke at 7:00, coded for 6h, skipped gym, ate instant ramen again, 3h YouTube. Entry 4 (Thu): Woke at 9:00, coded for 2h, gym finally (45min), ate salad, 0h YouTube. Entry 5 (Fri): Woke at 7:45, coded for 5h, skipped gym, instant ramen, 2h YouTube. Find the most surprising or noteworthy patterns and present them as structured findings a fun-fact writer could turn into something engaging.
probes.criteria:
The pattern_analyst agent reads provided data and identifies patterns, streaks, correlations, and surprising statistics in structured form. A GOOD response: (1) identifies at least 2-3 distinct patterns (e.g., ramen-eating correlates with skipping gym, coding hours inversely correlate with YouTube time, wake-time variability), (2) notes streaks (e.g., gym skipped 3/5 days, ramen 3/5 days), (3) highlights surprising correlations, (4) outputs findings in a clear structured format suitable for downstream fact-writer consumption.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #18 ('give me some interesting observation based on my jurnall'), #21 ('look for interesting stuff for yourself'), #23 ('look for interesting stuff there and share your observations').

probes.name: weekly_habit_streak_analysis
probes.prompt: Oto moje dane z ostatniego tygodnia. Chcę żebyś przeanalizował je pod kątem wzorców i serii. Poniedziałek: medytacja 10min, kawa 3 kubki, sen 6h, produktywność ocena 3/5. Wtorek: medytacja 10min, kawa 4 kubki, sen 5.5h, produktywność 2/5. Środa: brak medytacji, kawa 5 kubków, sen 5h, produktywność 2/5. Czwartek: medytacja 10min, kawa 2 kubki, sen 7h, produktywność 4/5. Piątek: medytacja 10min, kawa 3 kubki, sen 7.5h, produktywność 5/5. Sobota: brak medytacji, kawa 1 kubek, sen 9h, produktywność 1/5. Niedziela: brak medytacji, kawa 2 kubki, sen 8h, produktywność 3/5. Znajdź korelacje i ciekawe zależności i przedstaw je w ustrukturyzowanej formie.
probes.criteria:
The pattern_analyst agent reads provided data and identifies patterns, streaks, correlations, and surprising statistics. A GOOD response: (1) identifies the strong positive correlation between sleep and productivity, (2) notes the meditation streak (Mon-Tue, Thu-Fri) and its overlap with high-productivity days, (3) spots the inverse correlation between coffee intake and productivity, (4) flags Saturday as an anomaly (high sleep, low productivity — weekend effect), (5) presents findings structured for a fact-writer with clear labels like correlation type, strength, and narrative hooks.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #18 ('interesting observation based on my jurnall'), #22/#23 ('look for interesting stuff'), general pattern of the user asking for personal data pattern analysis.

probes.name: tech_usage_correlation_discovery
probes.prompt: I have been tracking my tech usage and side-project activity for the past 10 days. Here is the raw data: Day 1: 6h coding, 0 commits pushed, 2 YouTube videos watched, 0 blog posts read, mood: meh. Day 2: 4h coding, 1 commit pushed, 3 YouTube videos, 1 blog post, mood: good. Day 3: 8h coding, 3 commits pushed, 1 YouTube video, 0 blog posts, mood: great. Day 4: 2h coding, 0 commits pushed, 5 YouTube videos, 0 blog posts, mood: meh. Day 5: 3h coding, 0 commits pushed, 4 YouTube videos, 2 blog posts, mood: okay. Day 6: 7h coding, 2 commits pushed, 1 YouTube video, 1 blog post, mood: great. Day 7: 1h coding, 0 commits pushed, 6 YouTube videos, 0 blog posts, mood: bad. Day 8: 5h coding, 1 commit pushed, 2 YouTube videos, 1 blog post, mood: good. Day 9: 9h coding, 4 commits pushed, 0 YouTube videos, 0 blog posts, mood: great. Day 10: 3h coding, 0 commits pushed, 4 YouTube videos, 0 blog posts, mood: meh. Find me the most interesting patterns, correlations, and surprising statistics in this dataset. Focus especially on anything that could be turned into a fun or surprising observation.
probes.criteria:
The pattern_analyst agent reads gathered data and identifies patterns, streaks, correlations, and surprising statistics. A GOOD response: (1) identifies the strong positive correlation between coding hours and both commits and mood, (2) finds the strong negative correlation between YouTube watching and both coding hours and mood, (3) notes that 0 commits always coincide with 'meh' or 'bad' mood (100% pattern), (4) spots that blog posts were only read on medium-coding days (4-5h), (5) computes at least one surprising statistic (e.g., 'on days with 0 YouTube, average coding is 7.7h vs 2.3h on days with 4+ videos'), (6) structures output clearly for the fact-writer.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #17 ('pin point what should I analyse'), #20 ('a lot of possible interesting things there'), #31 ('fun cute pony fun facts'), #32 ('mining interesting process and correlation processes via llm').

probes.name: multivariate_daily_rhythm_analysis
probes.prompt: Zebrałem dane o swoim rytmie dnia przez dwa tygodnie. Oto one: D1: pobudka 6:00, śniadanie owsianka, praca 8h, sport tak (bieg 30min), ekran przed snem 1h, sen 7.5h. D2: pobudka 7:30, śniadanie jajecznica, praca 6h, sport nie, ekran 2h, sen 6h. D3: pobudka 6:30, śniadanie owsianka, praca 7h, sport tak (siłownia 1h), ekran 1h, sen 7h. D4: pobudka 9:00, śniadanie skip, praca 4h, sport nie, ekran 3h, sen 5.5h. D5: pobudka 6:00, śniadanie owsianka, praca 8h, sport tak (bieg 30min), ekran 0.5h, sen 8h. D6: pobudka 8:00, śniadanie kanapki, praca 5h, sport nie, ekran 2.5h, sen 6h. D7: pobudka 7:00, śniadanie jajecznica, praca 6.5h, sport nie, ekran 2h, sen 6.5h. D8: pobudka 6:00, śniadanie owsianka, praca 8h, sport tak (siłownia 1h), ekran 0.5h, sen 8h. D9: pobudka 6:15, śniadanie owsianka, praca 7.5h, sport tak (bieg 30min), ekran 1h, sen 7.5h. D10: pobudka 8:30, śniadanie skip, praca 4h, sport nie, ekran 3h, sen 5h. D11: pobudka 6:00, śniadanie owsianka, praca 8h, sport tak (siłownia 1h), ekran 0.5h, sen 8h. D12: pobudka 7:30, śniadanie kanapki, praca 6h, sport nie, ekran 2h, sen 6h. D13: pobudka 9:30, śniadanie skip, praca 3h, sport nie, ekran 3.5h, sen 5h. D14: pobudka 6:00, śniadanie owsianka, praca 8h, sport tak (bieg 30min), ekran 0.5h, sen 8h. Szukam wzorców, korelacji i niespodziewanych statystyk — co się rzuca w oczy? Przedstaw to w sposób gotowy do wykorzystania przez kogoś piszącego luźne ciekawostki o moim życiu.
probes.criteria:
The pattern_analyst agent reads gathered data and identifies patterns, streaks, correlations, and surprising statistics. A GOOD response: (1) identifies the strong cluster: owsianka breakfast + 6:00 wake-up + sport + 7.5-8h sleep + 8h work + low screen all co-occur (days 1,5,8,9,11,14 — a 6-day pattern), (2) spots that skipping breakfast perfectly correlates with wake-up after 8:30, below 5h work, 0 sport, and 3h+ screen time (days 4,10,13 — 100% match), (3) notes the streak of alternating 'good' and 'bad' rhythm days, (4) identifies at least one surprising stat (e.g., 'when you eat owsianka your average work hours are 7.9h vs 3.7h otherwise'), (5) outputs structured findings with narrative hooks for the fact writer, all in or respecting the Polish-language context of the prompt.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

probes.name: spending_and_mood_anomaly_detection
probes.prompt: Here is my spending and mood log from the past 12 days. Look for correlations, streaks, anomalies, and anything surprising. Day 1: spent 15 PLN (groceries), mood 4/5, ate at home. Day 2: spent 45 PLN (restaurant), mood 5/5, ate out. Day 3: spent 8 PLN (coffee), mood 3/5, ate at home. Day 4: spent 120 PLN (electronics), mood 4/5, ate at home. Day 5: spent 0 PLN, mood 2/5, ate at home. Day 6: spent 55 PLN (restaurant + dessert), mood 5/5, ate out. Day 7: spent 12 PLN (groceries), mood 3/5, ate at home. Day 8: spent 200 PLN (clothes), mood 4/5, ate at home. Day 9: spent 0 PLN, mood 1/5, ate at home. Day 10: spent 65 PLN (restaurant + drinks), mood 5/5, ate out. Day 11: spent 10 PLN (groceries), mood 3/5, ate at home. Day 12: spent 0 PLN, mood 2/5, ate at home. I want structured findings — what are the strongest patterns and what is the most counterintuitive thing here?
probes.criteria:
The pattern_analyst agent reads gathered data and identifies patterns, streaks, correlations, and surprising statistics. A GOOD response: (1) identifies the perfect correlation: every 'ate out' day has mood 5/5 (days 2,6,10 — 100% match), (2) identifies that 0 PLN spending days (5,9,12) have the lowest moods (2,1,2) forming a clear pattern, (3) notes that high one-off purchases (electronics 120, clothes 200) do NOT correlate with mood boost (both mood 4, same as a normal day), (4) spots the counterintuitive finding that spending more on food outside correlates with higher mood than spending more on non-food items, (5) computes at least one surprising stat (e.g., 'average mood on 0-spend days is 1.67 vs 4.0 on eat-out days'), (6) outputs findings clearly structured for the fact-writer agent.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #17 ('gather the most important and interesting data'), #32 ('mining interesting process and correlation processes via llm'), #39 ('pin point interesting topics'), and the general pattern of the user wanting personal-data pattern analysis with surprising/interesting angles.
